app.name: Copilot Cowork Estimator
app.tagline: Estimate Microsoft Copilot Cowork credits consumption
app.disclaimer: This tool produces indicative estimates. Values do not represent official or contractual Microsoft pricing.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.welcome: Welcome to the Copilot Cowork Estimator
dashboard.welcomeDesc: Create your first company to start estimating Copilot costs.

emptyState.description: Start by creating your first company and estimation scenario.

help.title: User Guide
help.subtitle: Everything you need to estimate Copilot Cowork credits.

intro.title: What this app is
intro.body: Copilot Cowork Estimator is a planning tool that estimates the consumption and cost of Copilot Credits for your organization. It runs entirely in the browser: no data is sent to external servers and everything is stored locally. Estimates are based on transparent, editable assumptions — not official Microsoft pricing.

quickstart.title: Quick start in 4 steps
quickstart.step1Body: Go to Companies and create a new client with its name and number of employees.
quickstart.step2Body: Start a new scenario, pick the company and define the workforce segments (e.g. Sales, IT, Managers).
quickstart.step3Body: For each segment set headcount, enabled and active user percentages, usage profile and preferred AI model.
quickstart.step4Body: The scenario automatically computes monthly/annual credits and cost as a min/mid/max range, with a per-segment breakdown.

concepts.title: Key concepts
concepts.creditsBody: The consumption unit of the Cowork model. Each task consumes credits based on model, context, tools and complexity.
concepts.rangeBody: Estimates are not a single number but a range. The midpoint is the main value; min and max show the best and worst case.
concepts.profileBody: Light, Medium and Heavy describe how intensively a segment uses Copilot each month. You can also create custom ones.
concepts.assumptionBody: A versioned set of calculation rules (credit bands, model factors). Kept separate from company data and updatable.

dataPrivacy.title: Data privacy
dataPrivacy.body: All data stays in your browser's localStorage. Export a JSON backup regularly from Settings to avoid losing your work: clearing browser data also deletes your simulations.

faq.a1: No. They are planning estimates based on assumptions. They do not represent a Microsoft quote or contractual price.
faq.a2: Exclusively in the browser (localStorage). No server, no login, no tracking.
faq.a3: Yes, via JSON export or (coming soon) a shareable link. The recipient can import the configuration.
faq.q4: How do I update calculation assumptions?
faq.a4: In the Assumptions section you can review and duplicate assumption packs to create custom versions.

welcome.title: Welcome to Copilot Cowork Estimator
welcome.body: Let's walk through the app in a few steps. You can skip the tour anytime and restart it from the Help page.

nav.title: Navigation
nav.body: From here you reach Dashboard, Companies, Scenarios, Assumptions and Compare. On mobile use the menu at the top left.

companies.title: Manage companies
companies.body: Start by creating a company with its employee count. Each company can have multiple estimation scenarios.

scenarios.title: Estimation scenarios
scenarios.body: A scenario models the workforce in segments and computes credits and costs as a range.

theme.title: Theme and language
theme.body: Switch between light/dark theme and English or Italian at any time.

help.title: Need help?
help.body: The full guide is always here. Look for the info icons next to values for quick explanations.

restartStep.title: Restart the tour anytime
restartStep.body: This button restarts the guided tour at any time, from any page. Once you've seen it, the tour won't start on its own again.

hints.monthlyCredits: Sum of estimated monthly credits across all active scenarios, for the selected range (min/mid/max).
hints.monthlyCost: Estimated monthly cost, obtained by valuing the credits at the funding plan's price per credit.
hints.annualCost: Annual projection of the monthly cost (× 12). It is an estimate, not a spending commitment.
hints.warnings: Number of functional warnings across scenarios: custom assumptions, exceeded budgets, incomplete data, etc.
hints.rangeSelector: Change the displayed value: Min (optimistic), Mid (expected), Max (conservative).
hints.enabledPct: Percentage of people in the segment assigned a Copilot license.
hints.activePct: Percentage of enabled users who actually use Copilot on a recurring basis.
hints.usageProfile: Average usage intensity of the segment: defines how many light/medium/heavy tasks each user runs per month.
hints.preferredModel: AI model used by the segment. More powerful models consume more credits per task.
hints.assumptionPack: Versioned set of calculation rules applied to the scenario. Determines credit bands and factors.
hints.headcount: Total number of people in this workforce segment.
hints.modelFactor: Multiplier reflecting the higher credit consumption of more capable models.
hints.creditBands: Estimated credits per task by intensity (light/medium/heavy), in min/mid/max values.
hints.costPerActive: Average monthly cost per user actively using Copilot.
hints.costPerEnabled: Average monthly cost per user assigned a license.
